ddls:
CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the transaction */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date when the transaction occurred */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount of the transaction */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method used for payment (e.g., credit card, PayPal) */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address for the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "membership_level" TEXT /* Membership level of the customer */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Current loyalty points balance */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer joined */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each item */,
  "item_name" TEXT /* Name of the item */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the item */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the item */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the item */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_items_category FOREIGN KEY ("category") REFERENCES item_category ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transaction_items" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transaction */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item purchased in the transaction */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the item purchased */,
  "unit_price" REAL /* Unit price of the item during the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id", "item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "association_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rule */,
  "antecedent_ids" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of item IDs representing the antecedents */,
  "consequent_ids" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of item IDs representing the consequents */,
  "antecedent_support" REAL /* Support for the antecedents */,
  "consequent_support" REAL /* Support for the consequents */,
  "support" REAL /* Support for the entire rule */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence of the rule */,
  "lift" REAL /* Lift metric of the rule */,
  "leverage" REAL /* Leverage metric of the rule */,
  "conviction" REAL /* Conviction metric of the rule */,
  "zhangs_metric" REAL /* Zhang's metric for the rule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_association_rules_antecedent_ids FOREIGN KEY ("antecedent_ids") REFERENCES items ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_association_rules_consequent_ids FOREIGN KEY ("consequent_ids") REFERENCES items ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule to which the metric applies */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_metrics_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES association_rules ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "item_support" (
  "support_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the support entry */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item */,
  "support_value" REAL /* Support value for the item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("support_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_item_support_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES items ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "item_category" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category, if applicable */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rule_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage event */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule used */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the marketing campaign */,
  "usage_date" TEXT /* Date when the rule was used */,
  "usage_type" TEXT /* Type of usage (e.g., recommendation, promotion) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_usage_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rule_usage_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES association_rules ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the campaign (e.g., demographics, interests) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_performance_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segmentation" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id", "customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "item_clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rule used to create the cluster */,
  "cluster_name" TEXT /* Name of the cluster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "item_cluster_items" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id", "item_id")
)
